_comment: Expected pass/fail counts per scenario as of 2026-04-28. test.fail() entries are tracked here so a future fix removing the annotation also updates this manifest. Run the suite, compare against this — anything inconsistent is a regression or a fix worth celebrating.

_legend.passes: Should pass on a green run.
_legend.expected_failures: Currently marked test.fail() — known open findings. Remove .fail() when the underlying fix lands and update this entry.
_legend.fixture_gaps: Test documents intent but cannot actually run yet — needs a dedicated fixture project / data state. Will throw early with an explicit error message so the gap is visible in the report.

passes: fresh login completes and lands on a logged-in route
navigation to /w/chat after login renders unified-UI shell
expected_failures: expired token should auto-redirect to /login (currently fails — finding #13)
logout button reaches login screen (currently fails — broken link)

passes: center panel mounts without long-running loading state
sidebar populates (or renders clean empty state) within 30s
expected_failures: right artifact panel exists with a toggle on /w/chat (currently fails — finding #14)

passes: typing and sending a message creates a thread + URL transitions
no "Request timed out" error appears after first send
thread title auto-names within 60s of first reply
S-UI-3._note: The Enter-submit path in helpers/chat.ts sidesteps the 1s AbortController race that finding #11 documented for click-submit. The watchdog test no longer expected-fails — it's now a regression detector.

expected_failures: Tier 1: project with full DB renders artifact from DB (currently fails — finding #4)
Tier 4: user explicitly requests modal → modal opens (currently fails — finding #4)
anti-fabrication: mismatched-name doc does NOT get scaled into the answer (currently fails — finding #10)
fixture_gaps: Tier 2: empty DB but populated DMS renders artifact extracted from docs (not yet implemented)
Tier 3: empty project renders explicit "not enough info" + no fabrication (currently fails — finding #4)

S-UI-8._note: The console-hygiene gate fails any other test that produces critical console errors. Counted as part of the wrapping test, not a separate scenario.
